description: Acute asthma exacerbation management in a child
system_instruction: Focus on pediatric asthma assessment, bronchodilator therapy, and severity classification. Communicate with both child and parent.

case_metadata.title: Acute Asthma Exacerbation in a Child

patient_persona.name: Liam Mitchell
patient_persona.background_story: History of asthma since age 3, triggered by viral infections and allergens

guardian.name: Sarah Mitchell
guardian.background_info: Concerned about son's worsening condition despite using inhaler at home

initial_prompt: My son has been coughing all night and now he's really struggling to breathe. His inhaler isn't helping like it usually does.

social_history.living_situation: Lives with parents and younger sister in pet-free home

physical_exam_findings.general: Young child sitting upright, appears distressed, using accessory muscles
physical_exam_findings.chest: Diffuse expiratory wheezing, prolonged expiratory phase, decreased air entry bilaterally, substernal and intercostal retractions

end_session.patient_response: Thank you doctor. I feel better now. Mom, can we go home?

critical_issue.response: Child's oxygen saturation dropping. Immediate escalation of therapy required.

evaluation_criteria.Severity_Assessment: Accurate classification of asthma exacerbation severity using pediatric scoring tools
evaluation_criteria.Bronchodilator_Therapy: Appropriate use and dosing of inhaled bronchodilators and systemic corticosteroids
evaluation_criteria.Oxygen_Therapy: Appropriate oxygen delivery and saturation monitoring
evaluation_criteria.Family_Communication: Effective communication with both child and parent
evaluation_criteria.Discharge_Planning: Appropriate discharge criteria, asthma action plan, and follow-up
evaluation_criteria.Preventive_Care: Assessment of controller medication adherence and trigger management

description: Viral gastroenteritis with dehydration assessment in a toddler
system_instruction: Focus on dehydration assessment, rehydration strategies, and monitoring in a young child.

patient_persona.name: Sophie Chen
patient_persona.chief_complaint: Vomiting and diarrhea for 2 days, decreased urine output
patient_persona.background_story: Started with vomiting 2 days ago, now has both vomiting and diarrhea. Daycare exposure to similar illness

guardian.name: Michelle Chen
guardian.background_info: Concerned about dehydration, has been trying to give Pedialyte but child vomits

initial_prompt: My daughter has been vomiting and has watery diarrhea for two days now. She's not keeping anything down and I'm worried she's getting dehydrated.

clinical_dossier.hidden_diagnosis: Acute Viral Gastroenteritis with Moderate Dehydration

history_of_presenting_illness.timing_and_duration: Vomiting started first, diarrhea started day 2
associated_symptoms: Decreased urine output (only 2 wet diapers in 24 hours)

past_medical_history: Up to date on immunizations including rotavirus

physical_exam_findings.HEENT: Dry mucous membranes, sunken eyes, normal fontanelles (closed)
physical_exam_findings.abdomen: Soft, mildly distended, hyperactive bowel sounds

end_session.patient_response: Thank you for taking care of my daughter. I understand the plan.

critical_issue.response: Child becoming lethargic. IV access and fluid resuscitation needed urgently.

evaluation_criteria.Dehydration_Assessment: Accurate clinical assessment of dehydration severity using validated pediatric scales
evaluation_criteria.Rehydration_Strategy: Appropriate fluid management - oral rehydration vs IV therapy
evaluation_criteria.Monitoring_Plan: Appropriate intake/output monitoring and reassessment schedule
evaluation_criteria.Parent_Education: Clear instructions on when to return and home management
evaluation_criteria.Preventive_Measures: Discussion of hygiene measures to prevent spread
evaluation_criteria.Medication_Management: Avoidance of inappropriate antidiarrheal or antiemetic use
